page.title: Medicare Prescription Drug Plans (Part D) | AltaMedicare
page.description: Medicare Part D explained: formularies, insulin caps, pharmacies, drug savings, and {year} Part D changes including the {partDAnnualCap} out-of-pocket cap.

header.ledeHtml: The right Part D plan can save you hundreds — or thousands — per year. Here’s how they work and how we shop for the best one for you.

how.intro: Medicare Part D is prescription drug coverage offered by private insurers. You can get Part D two ways:
itemsHtml: Standalone Part D plan — paired with Original Medicare or a Medicare Supplement
Built into a Medicare Advantage plan — called a Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug (MAPD) plan

choice.h2: Why Plan Choice Matters So Much
paragraphsHtml: Each Part D plan has its own formulary — the list of drugs it covers and how much they cost. The same medication can cost $4 on one plan and $140 on another. The right plan for you depends entirely on which specific drugs you take.
That’s why we always start by reviewing your medications before recommending anything. There’s no “best” Part D plan in general — only the best one for your specific drug list.

blocks.textHtml: The most important recent change: there’s now an annual cap on what you pay out-of-pocket for prescription drugs covered by Part D. Once you hit it, your covered drugs are $0 for the rest of the year. For people on expensive medications, this is a major change.
Insulin costs covered by Medicare are capped at {insulinCap} per month per prescription. No deductible applies to insulin.
You can choose to spread your annual prescription costs across monthly payments instead of paying a large bill at the pharmacy counter. Helpful if you have a high-cost specialty drug.

pharmacies.textHtml: Part D plans have preferred pharmacies that offer the lowest copays, and standard pharmacies that cost more. Many plans also have lower-cost mail-order options for maintenance medications. Where you fill matters as much as which plan you pick.

extraHelp.textHtml: If your income is below certain thresholds, you may qualify for Extra Help — a federal program that drastically lowers your Part D costs. Many people who qualify don’t know it. We can help you check.

callout.heading: Free prescription review.
callout.textHtml: Send us your medication list and we’ll tell you whether your current plan is your best option. No obligation. Start your prescription review →

cta.textHtml: One free, no-pressure call — clear answers, no sales pitch.
